meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Master Advanced Troubleshooting for Students
content.seo_description: Break through coding plateaus. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to debug complex apps and accelerate your development skills to a professional level.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for iterative debugging, you transform errors into learning opportunities. This workflow automates root-cause detection, allowing you to bypass manual trial-and-error and ship production-grade code faster while building a robust, high-value technical portfolio.
content.intro:
You’ve built the tutorials, followed the guides, and deployed the boilerplate. But now, you’re hitting the wall—that silent, soul-crushing moment where your logic fails and the terminal throws an error you don’t recognize. You aren't just stuck; you’re experiencing the critical transition from 'coder' to 'engineer.' The difference isn't knowing everything; it's knowing how to dismantle the unknown.

Today, we stop guessing. We are going to weaponize AI to dissect your codebase, turning your most frustrating bugs into a masterclass in software architecture. We’re moving beyond basic prompts into a professional-grade debugging loop that uses Antigravity to map dependencies and Claude Code to execute surgical fixes. Let’s turn your 'broken' project into your next portfolio breakthrough.

steps.title: Architectural Mapping with Antigravity
steps.description: Before fixing a bug, you must visualize the data flow. Use Antigravity to map your project structure and identify where the state management is leaking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project directory. Generate a dependency graph for the authentication flow and identify any circular imports or state inconsistencies.

steps.title: Context-Aware Error Injection
steps.description: Feed your error logs into Claude Code with the context of your recent file changes to pinpoint the exact commit that introduced the regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare the current broken build against the last stable state. Highlight the specific function call that is causing the memory leak.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'find the regression in src/hooks by comparing with git HEAD'

steps.title: Surgical Code Refactoring
steps.description: Instead of asking AI to 'fix it,' ask it to refactor the module to follow SOLID principles, which naturally resolves most hidden logic errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified faulty module to adhere to single-responsibility principles. Provide the diff for the refactor.

steps.title: Automated Test Generation
steps.description: Ensure the fix is permanent by generating unit tests that target the specific edge case that caused your initial crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite for the newly refactored module that covers the edge case of null-pointer exceptions in the API response handler.

steps.title: Verification and Documentation
steps.description: Validate the fix and document the resolution. This creates a technical audit trail for your portfolio, showing you understand the 'why' behind the 'fix'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the bug, the root cause identified, and the architectural fix implemented. Format this for a project README.

pro_tips: Always run your AI debuggers in a secondary branch to keep your main branch pristine.
Use Antigravity to identify 'Code Smells' before they manifest as runtime errors.
When Claude Code suggests a fix, force it to explain the logic behind the change to prevent 'hallucinated' solutions.
Treat your error logs as data; pipe them into a text file to provide Claude Code with long-form history rather than just the last line.
Use the 'diff' output from Claude to manually review changes, ensuring you understand the patch before committing.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your project, commit your changes, update your project documentation, and push to GitHub. Use the 'Bug Fix' summary generated in Step 5 as the basis for a LinkedIn post detailing your problem-solving process.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the toolkit to handle any technical debt that comes your way. Stop fearing errors and start treating them as the roadmap to your next promotion. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
